100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 25, 1926, British Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin announced a new coalition government, aiming to stabilize the nation amidst economic challenges. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Senate debated the Fordney-McCumber Tariff Act, which could significantly impact trade relations. In the realm of science, the world celebrated the successful launch of a new radio transmission that promised to enhance global communication.
